---
title: "Quarto Tips & Tricks"
subtitle: "Going further with Quarto"
sidebar: tips-sidebar
---

This section is for people who already know how to build Quarto documents and want to go further.
All three levels of this tutorial are built with the techniques described here.

::: {.callout-note}
## Official Documentation
The complete Quarto reference is at **[quarto.org/docs/reference](https://quarto.org/docs/reference/)** —
this section is a curated guide to the parts that are most useful in practice.
:::

---

## What's covered — and where to find it

| I want to… | Go to |
|---|---|
| Control output formats, freeze, and profiles | [The YML File](exp_yml.qmd) |
| Configure advanced sidebar sections and collapsing | [The YML File → Sidebar](exp_yml.qmd#sidebar) |
| Customise the site's look with themes and CSS | [The YML File → Styles](exp_yml.qmd#styles--css) |
| Control how code runs (`echo`, `eval`, `fold`, …) | [The QMD File](exp_qmd.qmd) |
| Show content only in certain profiles or formats | [The QMD File → Conditional content](exp_qmd.qmd#conditional-content) |
| Cross-reference figures, tables, and sections | [The QMD File → Cross-referencing](exp_qmd.qmd#cross-referencing) |
| Use quizdown, Pyodide, JSXGraph, or py-exercise | [Extensions](exp_extensions.qmd) |
| Serve the same project in multiple languages | [Multilanguage Projects](exp_multilanguage.qmd) |
| Look something up quickly | [Cheatsheet](exp_cheatsheet.qmd) |

---

## What you should already know

This section assumes you are comfortable with the following from the previous levels:

- Creating a Quarto **website**, **exercise sheet**, or **book** — [Getting Started](../../int/Qmd Files/Getting Started/getting_started.html)
- Running **Python in the browser** with Pyodide — [Extensions](../../int/Qmd Files/Getting Started/beg_extensions.html)
- What interactive Quarto output looks like — [Example Page](../../beg/Qmd Files/Example Page/index_beg.html)
